name: Saint Kitts and Nevis
avoid: May–November is warmer, wetter, and more humid, so ferry crossings, fort viewpoints, and the Nevis Peak trail are more likely to be disrupted by squalls or muddy conditions.
Cruise-heavy weekdays can make Basseterre, Romney Manor, and Brimstone Hill feel busier between late morning and mid-afternoon.
Nevis Peak is steep, rooty, and often slippery even in the dry season, so do not attempt it without a local guide and proper footwear.
stopoverNote: For a 4-6 day trip, split your stay between St. Kitts and Nevis instead of changing bases daily; Basseterre/Frigate Bay work well first, then take the ferry to Charlestown for the second half.

cities.name: Basseterre
cities.notes: Capital base for cruise-port walks, Brimstone Hill day trips, Romney Manor, ferry departures, and the easiest first-night logistics after landing on St. Kitts.

cities.name: Frigate Bay
cities.notes: St. Kitts' main resort strip for beach time, Timothy Hill views, scenic railway pickups, and easy taxi access back to Basseterre.

cities.name: Charlestown
cities.notes: Nevis base for ferry arrivals, Bath Hotel heritage stops, Pinney's Beach sunsets, and guided departures for the Nevis Peak trail.

itinerary.sim: Flow or Digicel prepaid data usually lands around ₹1,200–₹2,400 for enough maps, ferry coordination, and WhatsApp messaging across both islands; set it up on St. Kitts before island-hopping.

Basseterre.name: Basseterre
Basseterre.note: Stay near Basseterre or the airport corridor if you want the simplest arrival, fort day-trip, and ferry-terminal logistics before moving to a beach base.

days.theme: Historic Basseterre core, Romney Manor, and waterfront orientation

National Museum at Port Zante

activities.name: Romney Manor / Caribelle Batik gardens and workshop
activities.tip: Visitor admission is commonly listed around US$3, about ₹250.

activities.name: Port Zante boardwalk and ferry-terminal reconnaissance

days.theme: Brimstone Hill fortress day and western-island viewpoints

activities.tip: Adult admission is officially listed around US$15, which converts to about ₹1,245.

Frigate Bay.name: Frigate Bay
Frigate Bay.note: Use Frigate Bay if you want the classic resort stretch, easier beach time, and nightlife without being far from Basseterre's taxi and ferry links.

days.theme: Scenic Railway splurge, Timothy Hill views, and the beach strip

activities.name: St. Kitts Scenic Railway full island tour
activities.tip: Adult pricing is commonly published around US$129 for the classic sightseeing circuit, which converts to roughly ₹10,700.

activities.name: The Strip evening walk in Frigate Bay

Charlestown.name: Charlestown
Charlestown.note: Charlestown works best as your Nevis base because the ferry, heritage quarter, Bath Hotel ruins, and Pinney's Beach hotels are all close enough for short taxi rides.

days.theme: Charlestown heritage, Bath Hotel ruins, and Pinney's Beach

activities.name: Bath Hotel ruins and hot spring area
Alexander Hamilton Museum
activities.tip: Adult admission is commonly listed around EC$12, roughly ₹370.

activities.name: Charlestown waterfront and Memorial Square walk

days.theme: Guided Nevis Peak hike and a low-key recovery evening

activities.name: Nevis Peak guided hike
activities.tip: There is no formal park entry fee, but certified guide pricing for this demanding trail commonly falls around US$50–70 per person.

activities.name: Bath Stream or hotel pool recovery time
Charlestown evening taxi back from dinner

connections.method: Taxi or rental car via the southeast peninsula road (about 10-15m)
connections.cost: ₹1,150–₹1,550 per taxi; ₹3,700–₹6,200/day for a rental car

connections.method: Taxi back to Basseterre ferry terminal, then the public ferry to Charlestown, Nevis (about 60-90m door to door)
connections.cost: ₹775–₹1,085 pp by ferry, plus local taxi transfers

extras: December through April is the driest and most comfortable window, so it is the easiest season for fort views, ferry crossings, and the muddy Nevis Peak trail; book early because holiday demand raises room rates fast.
Official taxis on St. Kitts and Nevis do not use ride-hailing apps; agree the fare before you depart, especially on airport, beach-strip, and ferry-terminal runs.
The Basseterre-Charlestown ferry is the easiest island-to-island link for most travelers, while water taxis from the southeast peninsula are faster but cost materially more.

Rental cars are practical if you want Brimstone Hill, Romney Manor, Timothy Hill, and Pinney's Beach on your own schedule; drive on the left and budget about ₹2,000 extra for the local temporary driving permit.
Carry some cash in XCD for ferries, taxis, and smaller food stops even though resorts and larger restaurants usually accept cards.
